intended_use: Velacur is intended to provide estimates of tissue stiffness generated from shear wave speed measurements (40-70 Hz), ultrasound attenuation and Velacur Determined Fat Fraction (VDFF). The Velacur Det
intended_use_full: Velacur is intended to provide estimates of tissue stiffness generated from shear wave speed measurements (40-70 Hz), ultrasound attenuation and Velacur Determined Fat Fraction (VDFF). The Velacur Determined Fat Fraction combines ultrasound attenuation and backscatter coefficient measurements. The device is indicated to non-invasively determine liver tissue stiffness, attenuation, and Velacur Determined Fat Fraction. VDFF is not intended to be used in pediatric patients. These are meant to be used in conjunction with other clinical indicators in order to aid in clinical management of patients with liver diseases, including hepatic steatosis.
target_condition: liver diseases including hepatic steatosis, non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, non-alcoholic steatohepatitis
ground_truth: MRI proton density fat fraction (MRI-PDFF) for VDFF validation; manual image segmentation by experts in sonography and ultrasound elastography for organ guide and wave quality guide
other_metrics: Correlation coefficient (VDFF vs MRI-PDFF) in validation cohort: 0.85 [0.77-0.91]; Organ Guide Dice Coefficient > 0.7, Pixel accuracy > 80%; Wave Quality Guide Dice Coefficient > 0.7, Sensitivity and Specificity > 80%
